metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly specify that `CodeReviewRequest` gains a `session_id: str | None = None` field (alongside the already-listed `target_ticket`) so the full forwarding chain `_review_code → shim.review_code(session_id=…) → CodeReviewRequest(session_id=…) → gate_dispatch local-emit helper` is complete.
findings.finding: `CodeReviewRequest` dataclass has no `session_id` field, yet the plan's forwarding chain (`_review_code → shim.review_code → CodeReviewRequest → gate_dispatch local-emit helper`) requires it to carry `session_id` from the CLI layer into `gate_dispatch`. The plan adds `session_id` to `shim.review_code`'s signature and says it is 'forwarded onto `CodeReviewRequest`', but `CodeReviewRequest` (gate_dispatch.py:455-469) currently has no `session_id` field and the plan never explicitly names it as a field to add.
findings.location: What section / Scope / AC3 — `src/rebar/llm/workflow/gate_dispatch.py` CodeReviewRequest dataclass
findings.suggested_fix: Add one bullet to the Scope section: '`gate_dispatch.py` `CodeReviewRequest`: add `session_id: str | None = None` field (alongside `target_ticket`).' and mirror it in AC3: '`CodeReviewRequest` gains a matching `session_id` field'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add to AC6 a concrete proving command: name the test module path, the pytest invocation (e.g. `pytest tests/llm/test_local_review_artifact.py -v`), and include a cardinality assertion command (e.g. `assert len([t for t in rebar.list_tickets('code_review') if t['title'] == 'code-review: session:TEST']) == 1`) so two-run idempotency is machine-verifiable.
findings.finding: The Tests AC (AC6) names three test scenarios but provides no concrete proving commands — no test file path, no pytest invocation, and no assertion anchors (e.g. a jq cardinality check that the artifact count = 1 after two runs). A reviewer cannot confirm completion without knowing where to look or what to run.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC6 (Tests paragraph)
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC6: 'Verified by `pytest src/rebar/tests/llm/test_local_review_artifact.py -v`. The idempotency scenario asserts `len(matching_tickets) == 1` after two sequential calls with the same `REBAR_SESSION_ID`; the failure-swallow scenario asserts `review_exit_code == 0` after injecting a `create_ticket` exception.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify the plan: either add '--ticket' to the _review_code CLI argument scope, or explicitly state that the only work-ticket source is the commit's rebar-ticket: trailer (and remove the '--ticket' reference).
findings.finding: The plan's 'relates_to' bullet says the artifact links to 'the resolved work ticket (from --ticket or the head commit's rebar-ticket: trailer)', but _review_code in _cli/_llm_commands.py has no --ticket argument (lines 141-151), and the plan's Scope does not include adding one. Only the trailer path is actually available without additional CLI work.
findings.location: What section, 'relates_to link (best-effort)' bullet; Scope section listing _cli/_llm_commands.py
findings.suggested_fix: Remove 'from --ticket' from the relates_to bullet and rely solely on trailer extraction (which assemble.py already supports via commit_message), or add --ticket as an explicit scope item.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly state in the plan that revenued-thickset-dassie (the build_payload session_id extension) is a hard prerequisite and must land before this story, since build_payload currently does not read verdict.get('session_id').
findings.finding: The plan treats build_payload in sidecar.py as already reading verdict.get('session_id') — 'the payload (revenued-thickset-dassie's build_payload, which reads verdict.get("session_id")) carries it top-level' — but the live build_payload (sidecar.py lines 64-91) does not read or emit session_id at all. Revenued-thickset-dassie is an open, unimplemented story.
findings.location: What section, bullet 'Set session_id on the verdict': 'the payload (revenued-thickset-dassie's build_payload, which reads verdict.get("session_id")) carries it top-level'
findings.suggested_fix: Add 'Depends on: revenued-thickset-dassie (must be implemented first — build_payload does not yet read session_id)' to the Scope/Depends section, and change the present-tense assertion to future tense.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either (a) add a sequencing guard in AC1 ('deps map populated only once revenued-thickset-dassie ships') or (b) remove 'reviewed-file deps map' from AC1 if it is not part of this story's deliverable — the payload field must exist before the AC can be tested.
findings.finding: AC1 claims the emitted artifact carries 'the reviewed-file `deps` map', but `build_payload` in `src/rebar/llm/code_review/sidecar.py` has no `deps` field and no such field appears anywhere in the code-review verdict shape. The plan says it reuses the payload shape from 'revenued-thickset-dassie', which is not yet in the codebase.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria AC1 / src/rebar/llm/code_review/sidecar.py:64

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether `--ticket` is a new CLI argument to add to `_review_code` (and if so add it to the Scope changes), or remove `--ticket` from the `relates_to` description and rely solely on the `rebar-ticket:` commit trailer path.
findings.finding: The plan says the `relates_to` link resolves the work ticket 'from `--ticket` or the head commit's `rebar-ticket:` trailer', but `_review_code` currently accepts no `--ticket` CLI argument (confirmed by code inspection of `_llm_commands.py:129-184`). The plan scopes `_llm_commands.py` for changes and mentions `--ticket` as a ticket source but never specifies it as a new `argparse` argument to add — this is an undefined processing protocol whose input mechanism is unspecified.
findings.location: What section (`relates_to` link bullet) / Scope (`src/rebar/_cli/_llm_commands.py`) / AC5

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in the plan how the local-emit helper in `gate_dispatch.py` is gated to the local path only (e.g., an `emit_local: bool` flag or a separate local-only helper function), so the Gerrit voter path cannot trigger it and double-emit.
findings.finding: The plan adds a local-emit helper inside `gate_dispatch.py` but does not specify whether this helper is gated (called only on the local path) or unconditional. The Gerrit voter path also routes through `gate_dispatch` and already emits via `voter.emit_code_review_artifact`; if the new helper runs unconditionally, Gerrit invocations will double-emit a second session-keyed artifact and `verdict["session_id"]` may be set unexpectedly for the Gerrit payload.
findings.location: Scope / What — `gate_dispatch.py` local-emit helper; 'OUT of scope: the Gerrit voter path (already emits)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add an `emit_local_artifact: bool = False` parameter to `produce_code_review_verdict` (defaulting False, set True only by the local `_review_code` path). The helper block executes only when `emit_local_artifact=True`, making the Gerrit path immune by default.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify or document that the uniqueness/isolation guarantee for session keys is enforced by the harness (not by resolve_session_id itself), and add a note in the plan or test confirming what contract is actually relied upon.
findings.finding: The plan asserts resolve_session_id() is 'validated shared-across-subagents, unique-per-parallel-session', but session_id.py performs no such validation — it purely reads env vars via os.environ.get(). No test or doc verifies this property. The guarantee is entirely harness/environment-dependent, not enforced by the resolver itself.
findings.location: What section, bullet 'Resolve the local memory key': 'validated shared-across-subagents, unique-per-parallel-session'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit live/integration test criterion to AC6 (or cite existing voter integration coverage as a documented rationale for why offline tests are sufficient) to ensure the rebar.create_ticket / sidecar.emit / list_tickets boundaries are exercised end-to-end for the new default local-emit path.
findings.finding: AC6's test descriptions ('emits/reuses one session-keyed artifact', 'still persists') do not specify whether artifact-boundary calls (rebar.create_ticket, rebar.list_tickets, sidecar.emit) must be exercised live or may be satisfied by mocks. Since every local `review-code` invocation now routes through this new emit path by default, mock-only (proxy) validation could go green while the live path is silently broken (wrong ticket type, wrong sidecar payload, title mismatch in list scan).
findings.location: AC6 / Testing section of the plan
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC6: 'At least one test exercises the full emit path against a real (or test-harness-backed) artifact store — verifying that a `code_review` ticket with title `code-review: session:{id}` is actually created and that a second run reuses its id — OR document that the voter's existing integration tests provide equivalent live coverage of the same create-or-reuse logic.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether `--ticket` in the `relates_to` description is a new CLI argument added to `rebar review-code` (requiring argparse + scope update for `_llm_commands.py`) or whether ticket resolution is trailer-only (in which case remove `--ticket` from the prose and AC5 to avoid implementer confusion).
findings.finding: The plan's `relates_to` link mechanism mentions sourcing the work ticket from '`--ticket` or the head commit's `rebar-ticket:` trailer', but the Scope section lists `_llm_commands.py` only for 'resolve key, thread it down' — not for adding a `--ticket` CLI argument. If `--ticket` is a new flag, its argparse wiring is absent from scope; if it is NOT a new flag (trailer-only), the plan's prose is misleading and the shim/gate_dispatch signature carries an unused `target_ticket` concept that differs from the ticket-link concept.
findings.location: What section ('relates_to link (best-effort)') vs. Scope section (_llm_commands.py entry); AC5
findings.suggested_fix: If trailer-only is intended: replace 'from `--ticket` or the head commit's `rebar-ticket:` trailer' with 'from the head commit's `rebar-ticket:` trailer (parsed from `commit_message`, already threaded through `CodeReviewRequest`)' and update AC5 accordingly. If a CLI flag IS intended, add `_llm_commands.py` argparse change to the Scope bullet.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify whether the local-emit helper is invoked on INDETERMINATE/degraded verdicts (and if so whether `verdict['session_id']` is still stamped), or whether emit is skipped for non-PASS/BLOCK verdicts (mirroring `voter.emit_code_review_artifact`'s `if not verdict: return None` guard).
findings.finding: No fallback is specified for the INDETERMINATE/degraded verdict path. When `produce_code_review_verdict` returns a degraded INDETERMINATE verdict (outage, mid-run failure), the plan is silent on whether: (a) `verdict['session_id']` should still be stamped, and (b) the local-emit helper should still create/update the artifact. This is a missing sub-step fallback for a named failure mode that the existing gate already produces.
findings.location: What section (local-emit helper / best-effort) / AC6

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one test file path to the Scope section (e.g. `tests/llm/test_local_review_emit.py`) covering AC6's three scenarios: session-keyed reuse across two runs, bare-uuid persistence, and failure-swallowing.
findings.finding: AC6 describes a concrete test deliverable (session-keyed artifact across two runs, bare uuid run, failure-swallowing) but the Scope section lists no test files whatsoever, leaving the test work uncovered by any scoped file change.
findings.location: Scope section; AC6
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Scope: `tests/llm/code_review/test_local_emit.py` (new file; covers AC6 — session-keyed artifact reuse, uuid fallback persistence, best-effort swallow of artifact errors).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly specify in the plan that `verdict["session_id"] = session_key` is set BEFORE any `sidecar.emit` call (both the new local-emit path and the pre-existing `_run_code_review_gate` target_ticket emit path), and clarify whether the local-emit helper is an addition to or a replacement of the existing `request.target_ticket` emit branch.
findings.finding: The plan places `verdict["session_id"] = session_key` and the artifact create-or-reuse helper in `gate_dispatch.py`, but does not specify that this mutation must occur BEFORE the existing `sidecar.emit` call in `_run_code_review_gate` (line 594-596). The existing emit fires only when `request.target_ticket` is set — and the local path currently passes no `target_ticket` — but the plan's threading of `session_id` through `CodeReviewRequest` or via `gate_dispatch` leaves ordering ambiguous: if a future caller sets both `target_ticket` (Gerrit) and the session-key path triggers, `session_id` could be emitted twice or in the wrong sequence.
findings.location: What section ('Wire the local gate entry to emit + persist'); gate_dispatch.py `_run_code_review_gate` lines 582-599
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sentence under 'Wire the local gate entry': 'The `verdict["session_id"] = session_key` assignment happens immediately after the verdict is produced and before any `sidecar.emit` call; the local-emit helper is an ADDITIONAL emit path (the existing `request.target_ticket` branch in `_run_code_review_gate` remains for the Gerrit voter, which sets `target_ticket` from change-scoped artifact id — a disjoint path).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the ordering contract in the local-emit helper: `verdict['session_id']` must be stamped BEFORE the sidecar emit, and clarify whether the new local-emit helper REPLACES the existing `if request.target_ticket: sidecar.emit(...)` call in `_run_code_review_gate` or is additive.
findings.finding: The ordering of `verdict['session_id'] = session_key` relative to `sidecar.emit(verdict, ...)` is unspecified in the local-emit helper. `build_payload` (revenued-thickset-dassie) reads `verdict.get('session_id')` — so the stamp must happen BEFORE emit. But the existing `_run_code_review_gate` (line 593-595) already calls `sidecar.emit` when `request.target_ticket` is set; the plan layers a new local-emit helper into `gate_dispatch.py` but does not specify whether it replaces, wraps, or runs after the existing emit call, creating an ambiguous counter/state increment placement.
findings.location: What section (local-emit helper / `gate_dispatch.py`) / `src/rebar/llm/workflow/gate_dispatch.py:542-596`
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sentence: 'The local-emit helper replaces (not supplements) the existing `if request.target_ticket: sidecar.emit(…)` block in `_run_code_review_gate`; it stamps `verdict["session_id"] = session_key` first, then calls `sidecar.emit` once with `target_ticket=artifact_id`'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify that CodeReviewRequest.target_ticket already exists; the only required structural change is adding `target_ticket: str | None = None` and `session_id: str | None = None` to `shim.review_code`'s signature (and plumbing target_ticket into the CodeReviewRequest instantiation already present in the shim).
findings.finding: CodeReviewRequest already has a target_ticket field (gate_dispatch.py line 467); the plan's phrasing implies it needs to be added to CodeReviewRequest, which is misleading and may cause an implementer to add a duplicate field or a session_id field directly to CodeReviewRequest without realising target_ticket is already there.
findings.location: Scope section / What section — 'Shim signature change (in scope)' / gate_dispatch.py:467
findings.suggested_fix: In the Scope/What section, replace 'forwarded onto CodeReviewRequest(target_ticket=…, …)' with 'forwarded into the existing CodeReviewRequest(target_ticket=resolved_artifact_id, …) call in shim.py — CodeReviewRequest already carries target_ticket; no change to its dataclass definition is required.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in Scope whether `CodeReviewRequest` is defined inside `shim.py` or in a separate file (e.g. `code_review/models.py`), and if the latter, add that file explicitly to the Scope list.
findings.finding: The Scope section lists `shim.py` as the only file to modify for the signature change, but the plan also states parameters are 'forwarded onto CodeReviewRequest(target_ticket=…, …)'. If `CodeReviewRequest` is defined outside `shim.py` (e.g. in a models or request dataclass file), that file is not listed in Scope — an executor may miss it.
findings.location: Scope section — 'src/rebar/llm/code_review/shim.py (review_code: add target_ticket/session_id params)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a parenthetical: '(if CodeReviewRequest is defined in a separate models file, add that file to scope)'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a documentation step (ADR entry, CLAUDE.md section, or inline design note) capturing the local-emit session-keying contract and the create-or-reuse title format, so the reader/writer interface is discoverable without reading implementation code.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new architectural pattern — local `review-code` now emits persistent, session-keyed `code_review` artifacts via a create-or-reuse path — but specifies no ADR, CLAUDE.md update, or design-doc capture for this decision.
findings.location: ## What / ## Scope — overall plan; no documentation artifact mentioned anywhere
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Documentation' bullet to ## Scope: 'Update CLAUDE.md (or an ADR) to record the local-emit session-keying contract: artifact title format `code-review: session:{id}`, create-or-reuse idempotency semantics, and the relationship to the Gerrit voter path.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define a RETIRE/cleanup path for session-keyed code_review artifacts (e.g., a max-age TTL, an explicit delete after N days, or an archival policy) so they don't accumulate indefinitely.
findings.finding: The plan defines no RETIRE or cleanup path for session-keyed code_review artifacts. Every local review session mints or reuses a persistent artifact, and these accumulate indefinitely with no TTL, archival, or deletion policy.
findings.location: ## What — 'Create-or-reuse idempotency' bullet; ## Acceptance Criteria AC4
findings.suggested_fix: Add a brief policy statement: e.g., 'session-keyed artifacts older than 30 days are eligible for archival/deletion by the artifact GC job' (or whatever GC mechanism the system already uses). At minimum, document the expected accumulation rate and accepted upper bound.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Address the TOCTOU race in the create-or-reuse path — either rely on a server-side unique-title constraint and treat a conflict response as 'already exists', or document that duplicate artifacts are tolerated and the reader de-dupes by title, or add a note that parallel local reviews under one session are not a supported scenario.
findings.finding: The create-or-reuse logic (list tickets → find title match → create if absent) is a check-then-act TOCTOU race. Two concurrent local review invocations under the same session_id can both observe no existing artifact and both call create_ticket, producing duplicate tickets.
findings.location: ## What — 'Create-or-reuse idempotency' bullet; gate_dispatch.py local-emit helper
findings.suggested_fix: If rebar.create_ticket returns a conflict/duplicate error when a same-title artifact already exists, catch that error and re-fetch the artifact id (optimistic-create pattern). Otherwise, add a sentence: 'Concurrent local reviews under one session id are out of scope; callers must serialize or accept rare duplicate artifacts.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Consider extracting the shared create-or-reuse artifact core (`_resolve_or_create_code_review_artifact(title, description, *, repo_root)` → artifact_id) into `sidecar.py` or a shared helper, so both voter.py and the new local-emit path call the same function rather than duplicating the list_tickets scan + create_ticket sequence.
findings.finding: The plan's local-emit helper in `gate_dispatch.py` is specified to mirror `voter.py::emit_code_review_artifact` (lines 199–270) 'EXACTLY' — performing the same create-or-reuse scan (`list_tickets` → title match → `create_ticket`), `sidecar.emit`, and `relates_to` link sequence. This duplicates ~50 lines of production logic already in `voter.emit_code_review_artifact`, creating two divergence-prone copies of the same pattern rather than factoring the shared create-or-reuse core.
findings.location: What section — 'Create-or-reuse idempotency' bullet; Scope — gate_dispatch.py; voter.py lines 169–270
findings.suggested_fix: Extract a `_resolve_or_create_artifact(title, description, *, repo_root) -> str | None` helper in `sidecar.py` (or a new `src/rebar/llm/code_review/artifact.py`). Both `voter.emit_code_review_artifact` and the new local-emit helper call this shared function, then each appends the sidecar event and relates_to links with their own metadata. This keeps the idempotency logic in one place.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/_mcp_llm.py::review_code` to the Scope section (even if its treatment is 'deliberately not threaded — MCP callers have no session context') so the decision is explicit and the consumer is enumerated.
findings.finding: The MCP surface (`src/rebar/_mcp_llm.py::review_code`) calls `rebar.llm.review_code(...)` directly and is not listed in the Scope or out-of-scope sections. After the plan's changes, the MCP path will NOT emit session-keyed artifacts because it passes no session_id/target_ticket — an unenumerated consumer whose behaviour is left undefined.
findings.location: Scope section (out-of-scope list) / src/rebar/_mcp_llm.py:61

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly scope `sidecar.build_payload` in this story to emit `session_id: verdict.get('session_id')` (or confirm revenued-thickset-dassie is a hard prerequisite and state that ordering constraint in the Depends-on line).
findings.finding: The plan describes `build_payload` reading `verdict.get('session_id')` to carry `session_id` top-level in the payload (What section: 'The typed memory key for the reader is session:<session_key>'), but the current `build_payload` in `src/rebar/llm/code_review/sidecar.py` does not include a `session_id` field. The plan implies this shape extension comes from revenued-thickset-dassie, but no sequencing constraint is stated — if this plan ships first, `build_payload` must also be modified here.
findings.location: What section — 'Set session_id on the verdict' / src/rebar/llm/code_review/sidecar.py:64-91
findings.suggested_fix: Add `src/rebar/llm/code_review/sidecar.py` (`build_payload`: add `"session_id": verdict.get("session_id")`) to the Scope section, or add 'Blocked by revenued-thickset-dassie (must ship first)' to the Depends-on line.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Justify (or reconsider) placing the create-or-reuse local-emit helper inside gate_dispatch.py rather than in _llm_commands.py or a shared utility factored from voter.emit_code_review_artifact — the established pattern is that artifact creation is caller-side, not gate-engine-side.
findings.finding: The plan places the new create-or-reuse local-emit helper inside gate_dispatch.py (the pure gate engine), which is a novel architectural placement: the established codebase pattern is that artifact creation lives in the caller (voter.py), while gate_dispatch.py only calls sidecar.emit when a target_ticket is already resolved externally. No justification is given for why this higher-level artifact-management logic belongs in the gate dispatcher rather than in _llm_commands.py or a shared helper extracted from voter.emit_code_review_artifact.
findings.location: What section / Scope: 'gate_dispatch.py (local-emit helper: create-or-reuse the session artifact + set verdict["session_id"])' and 'Wire the local gate entry to emit + persist'
findings.suggested_fix: Move the local-emit helper to _llm_commands._review_code (or a new src/rebar/llm/code_review/local_artifact.py), where it sits at the same caller level as voter.emit_code_review_artifact. Alternatively, extract a shared create_or_reuse_code_review_artifact(title, ..., repo_root) utility from voter.py that both the local path and the Gerrit voter can call, eliminating the duplication the plan currently accepts.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an end-to-end AC (or extend AC1/AC4) with a concrete CLI proving sequence such as: `REBAR_SESSION_ID=e2e-test rebar review-code --diff <sample.diff> && REBAR_SESSION_ID=e2e-test rebar review-code --diff <sample.diff> && rebar list-tickets --type code_review | jq '[.[] | select(.title == "code-review: session:e2e-test")] | length == 1'` run against real (non-mocked) artifact storage.
findings.finding: No end-to-end proving command exercises the new local-emit code path against real artifact storage (live `rebar create_ticket` / `sidecar.emit`). All described verification is test-level; the plan never specifies a CLI invocation that proves the path works in a production-like environment, leaving the cutover unvalidated beyond mocked tests.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1, AC4; Scope section

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
